entities.name: Week 1 Upper Body Focus
entities.context: Day 1 of a structured weekly training program

entities.name: Tai Chi
entities.context: Listed as a warm-up option before deep breathing exercises

entities.name: Zone 2 Cardio
entities.context: Steady state moderate cardio at 65%-75% HR max, used in conditioning block

entities.name: Strength Circuit
entities.context: Core training method — exercises performed as a circuit for 1-3 rounds

entities.name: Bench Press
entities.context: Primary upper body push movement; alternatives include standard push-ups and barbell bench press at 65% load

entities.name: Chest Dips
entities.context: Upper body pressing exercise; substituted with close grip push-ups or 30° incline dumbbell chest press

entities.name: Seated Cable Row
entities.context: V-Bar close grip, pulled to lower abdomen at light weight; alternative is scapular retraction hold

entities.name: TRX Row
entities.context: Suspension trainer rowing movement, 3x10 or to failure

entities.name: Concept2 Rower
entities.context: Recommended equipment for optional Zone 2 conditioning work

entities.name: Dead Bug
entities.context: Core stability exercise performed 3x10 as part of the strength circuit

entities.name: Half-Kneeling Wood Chops
entities.context: Rotational core exercise using medicine ball; alternative is stack rope swing

entities.name: Scapular Retraction
entities.context: Movement pattern emphasized in cable row alternative — 10 second isometric hold

entities.name: Thoracic Spine Rotation
entities.context: Warm-up mobility drill, 1x8 per side, targeting upper back mobility

entities.name: Band Pull-Aparts
entities.context: Used in both warm-up (1x10) and strength circuit (20 reps) for posterior shoulder health

relationships.context: Day 1 is structured around a 1-3 round strength circuit as the main training block
Optional 15-20 min conditioning block post-strength work
Listed as one of the warm-up modality options
Rower is the suggested equipment for the Zone 2 conditioning block
Both are pressing movements sequenced together in the circuit
Scapular retraction hold is the alternative movement pattern for the cable row
Both are horizontal pulling movements in the same circuit, complementary back work
Both are core exercises closing out the strength circuit — anti-extension and rotation patterns
Both target upper back and shoulder health — warm-up mobility and postural endurance
